title: Carvana (CVNA) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Carvana (CVNA) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Projecting aggressive 15% FCF growth, assuming Carvana can maintain its newfound operational discipline while resuming market share expansion.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A high 12% discount rate is necessary given the extreme volatility, high leverage, and sensitivity to macroeconomic conditions.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3% terminal rate reflects the long-term growth potential in the massive, but highly cyclical, automotive retail market.

thesisTitle: The Remarkable Turnaround Priced to Perfection
thesisParagraphs: Carvana's survival and subsequent return to profitability is one of the most remarkable corporate turnarounds in recent memory. By slashing costs, optimizing logistics, and restructuring its debt, the company proved that its e-commerce model can generate positive unit economics.

However, the current valuation appears to price in a flawless execution path forward. At these levels, the market is assuming Carvana will rapidly expand market share while maintaining its peak margins, a daunting task in a highly competitive, rate-sensitive industry. While the business is fundamentally stronger, the stock appears overextended.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: How did Carvana avoid bankruptcy?
faq.answer: Through aggressive cost-cutting, structural improvements in per-unit profitability, and a crucial debt restructuring deal with its major bondholders.

faq.question: Why use a 12% discount rate?
faq.answer: The high rate reflects the inherent risks of Carvana's business model, including sensitivity to interest rates, used car price volatility, and high debt levels.

faq.question: Can Carvana sustain its current margins?
faq.answer: That is the key debate. While operational efficiencies are likely permanent, GPU could be pressured by increased competition or a deteriorating macroeconomic environment.
